protocol.name: wlr_output_power_management_unstable_v1

interface.name: zwlr_output_power_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to create per-output power management

interface.description: This interface is a manager that allows creating per-output power management objects. It is only present when the compositor supports the corresponding feature.

request.name: get_output_power

description.summary: get a power management object for an output

request.description: Create an output power management object for the given output. The output object allows the client to control the power state of the output.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the manager. This request does not affect any existing output power management objects.

interface.name: zwlr_output_power_v1

description.summary: adjust power management for an output

interface.description: This object offers requests to set the power management mode of an output.

entry.name: on
entry.summary: Output is turned on, no power management

request.name: set_mode

description.summary: set an output's power save mode

request.description: Set an output's power save mode to the given mode. The mode change is effective immediately. If the output does not support the given mode a failed event is sent.

arg.name: mode
arg.summary: the power save mode to set

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the output power management object. This does not affect the output's power management mode.

event.name: mode

description.summary: report a power management mode change

event.description:
Report the power management mode change of an output.
The mode event is sent after an output changed its power management mode. The reason can be a client using set_mode or the compositor deciding to change an output's mode. This event is also sent immediately when the object is created so the client is informed about the current power management mode as soon as it sets up the object.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the output power management object is no longer valid. This can happen for a number of reasons, including: - The output disconnected - The output does not support power management - Another output power management object already exists for this output
The client should destroy this object.
